contraindications.reason: History of hypersensitivity to secukinumab or any component
Can impair immune response; risk of worsening infection
Screen for TB; cases of reactivation reported with IL-17 inhibitors; treat latent TB before starting
Secukinumab may exacerbate Crohn's disease; use with caution in inflammatory bowel disease
Avoid live vaccines during therapy
History of hypersensitivity to calcipotriene or any component
Topical calcipotriene can be absorbed and may cause hypercalcemia; contraindicated in patients with known disorders of calcium metabolism
Increased risk of hypercalcemia due to reduced calcium excretion
May cause irritant dermatitis on sensitive facial skin; avoid or use with caution

Pregnancy Category X; known teratogen, causes severe birth defects; must not be used in pregnant women or women who may become pregnant; effective contraception required before, during, and for 3 years after therapy
Excreted in breast milk; potential for serious adverse effects in nursing infants
History of hypersensitivity to acitretin, other retinoids, or any component
Hepatotoxic potential; contraindicated in severe liver disease
Risk of drug accumulation and toxicity due to renal excretion
Increased risk of hepatotoxicity when combined with methotrexate
Concomitant use increases risk of benign intracranial hypertension (pseudotumor cerebri)

contraindications.contraindication_name: blood donation during and for 3 years after therapy
contraindications.reason: To prevent potential teratogenic exposure to blood recipients
History of hypersensitivity to betamethasone, other corticosteroids, or any component

contraindications.contraindication_name: untreated bacterial, fungal, or viral skin infections
contraindications.reason: Topical corticosteroids can mask or exacerbate infections and delay healing
May worsen these conditions; avoid application to face in these disorders

contraindications.contraindication_name: skin atrophy or impaired circulation in the application area
contraindications.reason: Corticosteroids can cause further skin thinning and delayed healing
History of hypersensitivity to betamethasone, calcipotriene, any corticosteroid, vitamin D analog, or any component
Calcipotriene absorption may exacerbate hypercalcemia

Cyclosporine can elevate blood pressure; hypertension should be controlled before initiation
Immunosuppression can worsen infections
Nephrotoxic; requires careful dose adjustment and monitoring of renal function
Increased risk of malignancy, particularly lymphoma, with prolonged immunosuppression
Avoid live vaccines during immunosuppressive therapy
Grapefruit juice inhibits CYP3A4 metabolism, increasing cyclosporine blood levels and toxicity risk

Excreted in breast milk; may cause serious adverse reactions in nursing infants
History of hypersensitivity to methotrexate or any component
Renal elimination is major route; accumulation leads to severe myelosuppression and toxicity
Hepatotoxic potential; avoid in significant liver disease or alcoholism
Immunosuppression can worsen infections

contraindications.reason: Myelosuppressive effects can be life-threatening in patients with pre-existing bone marrow failure
Third-space fluid collections delay methotrexate clearance, increasing toxicity risk
Avoid live vaccines during immunosuppressive therapy
History of hypersensitivity to tacrolimus or any component

contraindications.contraindication_name: active viral skin infections (e.g., herpes simplex, varicella)
contraindications.reason: May potentiate viral replication

contraindications.contraindication_name: Netherton's syndrome or similar skin barrier defects
contraindications.reason: Increased systemic absorption risk leading to immunosuppression and toxicity
Limited data; potential fetal risk due to systemic absorption; avoid unless clearly needed
Systemic absorption possible; caution in nursing

contraindications.contraindication_name: concurrent use of PUVA or UVB therapy
contraindications.reason: Potential increased risk of skin malignancy
May promote tumor growth due to local immunosuppression
History of hypersensitivity to calcitriol, other vitamin D analogs, or any component
Topical vitamin D analog can be absorbed and may increase serum calcium; contraindicated in patients with disorders of calcium metabolism
Decreased calcium excretion increases risk of hypercalcemia
May cause irritation on sensitive facial skin
History of hypersensitivity to coal tar or any component

contraindications.reason: Can cause severe irritation and increased systemic absorption
Limited data; potential risk due to polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons; topical application should be limited to small areas

contraindications.reason: Coal tar contains carcinogens; avoid use on malignant or pre-malignant lesions
